author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
A Summary Recapitulation of the Errors of Victor.
title: A Summary Recapitulation of the Errors of Victor.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: Chapter 22 [XV.]—A Summary Recapitulation of the Errors of Victor.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: What these particular errors are, I have, to the best of my ability, already explained.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: But I will run over them again with a brief recapitulation.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: One is, “That God did not make the soul out of nothing, but out of His own self.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: A second is, that “just as God who gives is Himself ever existent, so is He ever giving souls through infinite time.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: The third is, that “the soul lost some merit by the flesh, which it had had previous to the flesh.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: The fourth is, that “the soul by means of the flesh recovers its ancient condition, and is born again through the very same flesh by which it had deserved to be polluted.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: The fifth is, that “the soul deserved to be sinful, previous to any sin.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: The sixth is, that “infants which are forestalled by death before they are baptized, may yet attain to forgiveness of their original sins.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: The seventh is, that “they whom the Lord has predestinated to be baptized may be taken away from his predestination, or die before that has been accomplished in them which the Almighty has predestined.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: The eighth is, that “it is of infants who are fore-stalled by death, before they are born again in Christ, that the Scripture says, ‘Speedily was he taken away, lest wickedness should alter his understanding,’” with the remainder of the passage to the same effect in the Book of Wisdom.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: The ninth is, that “there are outside the kingdom of God some of those mansions which the Lord said were in His Father’s house.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: The tenth is, that “the sacrifice of Christians ought to be offered in behalf of those who have departed out of the body without being baptized.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: The eleventh is, that “some of those persons who have departed this life without the baptism of Christ do not in the meanwhile go into the kingdom, but into paradise; afterwards, however, in the resurrection of the dead, they attain even to the blessedness of the kingdom of heaven.”
